blocks.text: caught it. The yes had come fast. She didn't say anything about it — just turned back to Marcus like the answer had always been yes.
was already looking back at the fire when Marcus finished talking. The group. The whole setup. He just wanted to be in it.
didn't examine it. He just said yes and meant it.
understood the yes. She nodded at Marcus like it had already been decided.

blocks.content: The beach was better than I expected. I didn't tell her that. She would have made it a moment and it didn't need to be a moment — it was just good.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: This is actually incredible. Look at how far out the water goes. I can't see the end of it.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: It's the Atlantic. The end is Portugal.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: I know what ocean it is, Julian.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: I'm saying it looks different when you're standing in front of it. Don't give me geography.
She was right about that too. It did look different. I spent a few seconds just watching it and didn't think about anything else.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: You were somewhere else for a second. Just now. That was good — that was you actually being here.

blocks.prompt: She saw something in him that she was glad to see. What does he do with that?

options.label: He looks at her instead of the water. Just for a second.
He stays with the water. It's easier and it's actually good.
"You're keeping score." He almost smiles when he says it.
